prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `e633a9e5`.
codex_response:
Implemented `e633a9e5` as a new ARC2 task package. The rule is the same as the official puzzle: expand a dense `3x3` grid to `5x5` by duplicating the outer rows and outer columns while keeping the middle row and middle column single. The summary hint was used because it matched the official examples exactly.

The task package is in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_e633a9e5/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_e633a9e5/verifier.py), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_e633a9e5/generator.py). No new primitive was needed. The generator matches the family by producing dense nonzero `3x3` color grids with moderate color repetition, then deriving the `5x5` output by the same outer-row/outer-column duplication structure seen in the reference task.

Validation passed end to end: official examples `4/4`, generated examples `1000/1000`, and the artifacts were built at [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/e633a9e5/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/e633a9e5/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/e633a9e5/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/e633a9e5/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/e633a9e5/previews/preview_03.png). I also visually checked [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/e633a9e5/previews/preview_01.png); the examples look on-distribution.
